input.prompt:
I just upgraded my .NET MAUI app from .NET 9 to .NET 10. On iOS, my content
is now going behind the status bar and the Dynamic Island notch area. In .NET 9
I was using `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` and it worked fine. What's the correct
way to handle this in .NET 10? Show me the XAML.

data.content:

input.initialPrompt:
I just upgraded my .NET MAUI app from .NET 9 to .NET 10. On iOS, my content
is now going behind the status bar and the Dynamic Island notch area. In .NET 9
I was using `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` and it worked fine. What's the correct
way to handle this in .NET 10? Show me the XAML.

data.content:
In .NET 10 MAUI, the iOS-specific `ios:Page.UseSafeArea` was replaced with a cross-platform `Page.IgnoreSafeArea` property (defaults to `false`, meaning safe area is respected). However, if your content is going behind the status bar/notch, you likely need to explicitly opt in using the new API:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage">

    <Grid Padding="{OnPlatform iOS='0,47,0,34'}">
        <!-- your content -->
    </Grid>
</ContentPage>
```

**Or**, use the new cross-platform replacement — set `IgnoreSafeArea` to `false` (which should be the default):

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             IgnoreSafeArea="False">
    <!-- content stays within safe area -->
</ContentPage>
```

**Key changes from .NET 9 → 10:**

| .NET 9 | .NET 10 |
|--------|---------|
| `xmlns:ios="clr-namespace:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific;..."` | No longer needed |
| `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` | `IgnoreSafeArea="False"` (default) |

If the default isn't working, check that you haven't set `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on a parent layout, and ensure your `Shell` or `NavigationPage` isn't overriding the behavior. You can also remove any leftover `ios:` namespace references — the old attached property is no longer recognized and may silently fail.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI migration issue from .NET 9 to .NET 10 regarding safe area handling on iOS. This is a known breaking change in .NET 10 MAUI.

In .NET 10, the `ios:Page.UseSafeArea` attached property was removed/deprecated. The new approach uses padding or the `SafeAreaRegions` property.

Actually, let me recall the details. In .NET 10 (which would be the next version after .NET 9), Microsoft introduced changes to how safe areas work in MAUI. The `UseSafeArea` platform-specific was replaced with a cross-platform `Page.SafeAreaRegions` property.


The replacement uses a cross-platform `SafeAreaRegions` property that accepts values like `None`, `All`, or specific regions, though I want to verify the exact API details since the specifics around .NET 10's implementation are still coming into focus. The `SafeAreaRegions` property accepts an enum with values like `None`, `All`, `Top`, `Bottom`, `Left`, and `Right`, giving developers fine-grained control over which safe area insets to respect across all platforms that support them.
